title: A not descriptive error message when value of BLOB is passed to JSON function: Malformed JSON at byte 0 of input: input length is 0.  Input:
description:
### What happens?

This error is raised when calling json function on a value of type BLOB. `duckdb.duckdb.InvalidInputException: Invalid Input Error: Malformed JSON at byte 0 of input: input length is 0.  Input:`. I expect a more descriptive error.

I encountered this when I needed to get JSON from packed values from many columns `json(struct_pack(*columns(*))))`. Initially, I didn't understand why it says length 0. Eventually I found out that 2 columns of types BLOB and BLOB[] caused this.

### To Reproduce

```
select json(from_hex('aa'));
```

### OS:

NixOS, Linux 6.1.55, x86_64

### DuckDB Version:

1.2.1

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Dacos Nguyen

### Affiliation:

Freelancer

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, data BLOB, json_data JSON);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, data) VALUES (1, UNHEX('aa'));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, data) VALUES (2, NULL);
SELECT JSON_ARRAY(data) FROM test_table WHERE id = 1;
SELECT JSON_ARRAY(data) FROM test_table WHERE id = 2;
key_bug_features: Calling JSON function on BLOB type values
